title: centaurea setosa
intro: Centaurea setosa, also known as yellow knapweed or rough knapweed, is a perennial plant in the aster family native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. Centaurea setosa can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and respiratory issues.
